text: When patients receive a cancer diagnosis, they are faced with making a series of critical and potentially difficult decisions regarding their treatment. This chapter reports on an interdisciplinary effort to generate human readable, individualised listings of the characteristics of treatment options that may aid patients in these decisions. Using the cancer registries (Netherlands Cancer Registry) and profiles datasets (PROFILES) of the Netherlands Comprehensive Cancer Organisation (IKNL), we gathered individualised clinical statistics per type of cancer for multiple tumour sites and potentially relevant non-cancer variables. These data were combined with patient-generated descriptions and quotations per tumour site to create an informative base to support patients and their doctors during the process of sharing information when deciding which treatment to undergo. We reflect on our research and the contribution it makes, both in terms of understanding the impact of our research and its generalisability to other healthcare contexts and patient health decisions.
headline: Fully Human Written
